meanings.urdu: Kam karna (shiddat ya takleef ko) - کم کرنا
meanings.hindi: Kam karna / Ghatana - कम करना / घटाना

examples: The government planted trees to mitigate the effects of climate change.
She took some medicine to mitigate her severe headache.
Good communication can mitigate misunderstandings in the workplace.
We need to design a system to mitigate the risks of failure.
His apology did little to mitigate her anger.

meanings.urdu: Hairan karna / Uljha dena - حیران کرنا / الجھا دینا
meanings.hindi: Bhramit karna / Chakma dena - भ्रमित करना / चकमा देना

examples: The complex puzzle continued to confound the students.
His strategy was to confound his enemies by doing the unexpected.
The magician's tricks never fail to confound the audience.
Don't confound the issue by bringing up irrelevant details.
The results of the experiment completely confounded the scientists.

meanings.urdu: Na-guzeer / Bohat zaruri - ناگزیر / بہت ضروری

examples: Mobile phones have become indispensable in our daily lives.
She is an indispensable member of the team; we cannot work without her.
Water is indispensable for the survival of all living things.
A good dictionary is indispensable for learning a new language.
Honesty is considered an indispensable quality in a leader.
The best thing about this apartment is its proximity to the park.
We chose the hotel because of its proximity to the airport.
The proximity of the exams made the students nervous.
Avoid close proximity to people who have the flu.
The two houses are built in close proximity to each other.
He is a diligent student who always finishes his homework on time.
The lawyer was diligent in preparing the case for his client.
Success usually comes to those who are diligent and persistent.
She made a diligent effort to learn the guitar.
The company rewarded the employee for his diligent service.

meanings.urdu: Zaroorat se ziyada / Faltu - ضرورت سے زیادہ / فالتو

examples: Please avoid superfluous details and get to the point.
He cleared out all the superfluous items from his garage.
The report was full of superfluous information that confused everyone.
Wearing a heavy coat in summer is completely superfluous.
Since the meeting was cancelled, my preparation was superfluous.

meanings.urdu: Mazay lena / Lutf uthana - مزے لینا / لطف اٹھانا
meanings.hindi: Swad lena / Anand lena - स्वाद लेना / आनंद लेना

examples: She closed her eyes to savour the taste of the chocolate.
He wanted to savour the moment of victory with his team.
We sat on the balcony to savour the cool evening breeze.
Don't rush through life; learn to savour the small joys.
I always savour my morning coffee before starting work.

meanings.urdu: Wazeh / Roshan / Zinda - واضح / روشن / زندہ
meanings.hindi: Spasht / Jeevant / Chamkila - स्पष्ट / जीवंत / चमकीला

examples: I have a vivid memory of my childhood home.
The artist used vivid colors to paint the sunset.
She gave a vivid description of the accident.
He has a vivid imagination and writes great stories.
The dream was so vivid that I felt like it was real.

meanings.urdu: Lachak / Himmat na haarna - لچک / ہمت نہ ہارنا

examples: The resilience of the human spirit is amazing during disasters.
Building resilience helps you cope with stress and failure.
The team showed great resilience to win the game after being down.
Rubber has high resilience and returns to its shape after stretching.
She praised her son for his resilience in recovering from illness.

meanings.urdu: Tang aa jana / Jhunjhlana - تنگ آ جانا / جھنجھلانا

examples: He let out an exasperated sigh when the computer crashed again.
The teacher became exasperated with the noisy students.
She looked exasperated after waiting in line for two hours.
I am completely exasperated by his constant excuses.
It is easy to feel exasperated when things don't go as planned.
